senses.definition: A shortened or informal form of the word 'scat', meaning feces or excrement in some dialects, or an exclamation used to urge someone to go away; not common in standard English.
examples: This is a slang term; you won't hear it in polite conversation.
The phrase 'scatt!' is sometimes heard in old minstrel shows.
In some dialects, 'scatt' can refer to waste or refuse.

senses.definition: Past tense of 'scatter', meaning to throw in various directions or to spread over an area.
examples: The seeds were scattered across the field.
She scattered the papers on the desk.

senses.definition: To throw or drop things in different directions so that they spread over an area.
examples: He scattered the cards on the table.
Flowers were scattered along the path.

senses.definition: A dispersion or small pieces spread over an area.
examples: There was a scatter of rocks along the shore.
The scatter of colors across the painting was deliberate.

senses.definition: Capable of being scattered or dispersed.
examples: The seeds are scatterable by hand.
Not all materials are easily scatterable.

senses.definition: The act or process of scattering; a dispersion.
examples: The scatteration of seeds helped the field recover.
Scientists studied the scatteration pattern of the material.

senses.definition: Plural of scatteration; multiple instances of scattering or dispersions.
examples: Different scatterations were observed in the samples.
We recorded several scatterations during the experiment.

senses.definition: To scatter away; to cause to disappear by dispersing.
examples: The birds scatters away when the hunter approaches.
Scatteraway the crowd to clear the exit path.

senses.definition: A person who is forgetful, absent-minded, or not very careful.
examples: Don't be a scatterbrain and write down the appointment.
He's a friendly scatterbrain who often loses his keys.

senses.definition: Having a forgetful or disorganized way of thinking.
examples: Her scatterbrained habit made her lose track of time.
He can be scatterbrained but is very kind.

senses.definition: Plural of scatterbrain; multiple forgetful or absent-minded people.
examples: The group was full of scatterbrains trading ideas.
We joked that they were scatterbrains but talented.

senses.definition: Spread out in many places; not in a neat group.
examples: The toys were scattered all over the floor.
The city had scattered houses around the hills.

senses.definition: In a scattered or spread-out manner.
examples: The leaves lay scatteredly across the yard.
Bits of paper were scatteredly arranged on the desk.

senses.definition: The state of being scattered or dispersed; lack of order.
examples: The scatteredness of the data made analysis difficult.
There was a sense of scatteredness after the event.

senses.definition: Something or someone that scatters things or causes dispersion.
examples: A wind scatterer blew seeds across the meadow.
The scatterer of light reduced glare.

senses.definition: Plural of scatterer; multiple sources of dispersion.
examples: The scatterers in the experiment were calibrated.
We identified several scatterers in the dataset.

senses.definition: A surname or proper noun; also used as a name in fiction; not a common general term.
examples: I met Mr. Scattergood at the conference.
Scattergood is a character in the novel.

senses.definition: Unclear or obscure term; likely a playful or historical plural; not widely used in contemporary English.
examples: The term 'scattergoods' appears in old texts and is seldom used today.

senses.definition: A type of graph showing the distribution of two variables; similar to a scatter plot.
examples: The scattergram shows the relationship between height and weight.
Scientists used a scattergram to visualize data points.

senses.definition: Plural of scattergram; multiple scattergram graphs.
examples: The report includes several scattergrams for different datasets.

senses.definition: A type of graph used to show relationships between two variables; another term for scatterplot.
examples: We analyzed the scattergraph to interpret trends.

senses.definition: A shotgun with a broad spray of shot; also used metaphorically to mean broad or unfocused in approach.
examples: He fired a scattergun blast of questions.
The policy adopted scattergun proposals.

senses.definition: Plural of scattergun; multiple shotgun devices or broad approaches.
examples: The team used scatterguns to cover different angles.

senses.definition: The act of dispersing things or people; the process of spreading out.
examples: The scattering of the crowd caused confusion.
The scattering of seeds helps plants grow.

senses.definition: Present participle of scatter; to throw or spread things in different directions.
examples: Wind was scattering leaves across the yard.
They were scattering the flyers outside the theater.

senses.definition: In a scattered or dispersed manner; not in an orderly way.
examples: Notes were scatteredly arranged on the desk.
The pages lay scatteredly around the room.

senses.definition: Plural of scattering; instances where things are dispersed.
examples: There were several scatterings of seeds.
We observed multiple scatterings in the data.
